{{Infobox museum | name = Shima City Museum of History and Folklore (志摩市歴史民俗資料館) | image = [[File:Shima City Museum of History and Folklore.jpg|280px]] | native_name = 志摩市歴史民俗資料館 | established = July 24, 2012 (as Shima City Museum of History and Folklore)
April 1, 1960 (as Isobe Town Library) | dissolved = | location = 878-9 Hazama, Isobe-cho, {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Japan | latitude = 34.3755 | longitude = 136.7970 | type = Local History Museum | owner = {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}} | operator = Shima City Board of Education | director = Yumiko Sakigawa丸山崇志「ダウン症の人の絵添えて 各地のおひなさま展 志摩」2014年2月19日付朝刊、伊勢志摩版14ページ | map_type = Japan Mie | website = [http://www.city.shima.mie.jp/kurashi/kyouiku/post_265/ Official Website] }} [[File:Obaachan-no-ie.jpg|thumb|Inside the former Shima City Isobe Local Museum ("Grandmother's House").]] The '''Shima City Museum of History and Folklore''' ({{ill|Shima City Museum of History and Folklore|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}}) is a local history museum in {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Japan. This article also covers the adjacent facilities: the '''Shima City Isobe Library Room''', the '''Shima City Isobe Lifelong Learning Center''', the '''Isobe Branch of Shima City Hall''', and the predecessor museum, the '''Shima City Isobe Local Museum.''' == Overview == The facility integrates four functions into a single building: the History and Folklore Museum, Isobe Library Room, Isobe Lifelong Learning Center, and Isobe Branch of Shima City Hall. The building was initially planned as a new town hall for {{ill|Isobe, Mie|ja|磯部町 (三重県)}} before the town's merger into Shima City in 2005. It was completed on February 22, 2005.志摩市企画広報課 編（2005）：10ページ === History and Folklore Museum === Located on the north side of the first floor (to the left of the main entrance). The museum's purpose is to "preserve the lifestyle and culture of Shima and display its treasures as a shared asset for future generations."西城利夫「ふるさと再発見 志摩市歴史民俗資料館と志摩国 自然と人の暮らしを紹介」[[中日新聞]]2012年8月18日付[[朝刊]]、伊勢志摩版16ページ It features five sections: archaeology, festivals (folklore), daily life (tools), livelihoods (work), and reminiscence therapy (Showa-era life).{{Cite web|url=https://megalodon.jp/2012-0816-1714-57/mainichi.jp/area/mie/news/20120725ddlk24040284000c.html|title=志摩市歴史民俗資料館:開館 考古など5コーナー 海の恵みテーマ、きょうから企画展/三重|publisher=[[毎日新聞]]|date=2012-07-25}} At its opening, a special traveling exhibit from the Mie Prefectural Museum, titled "Blessings and Vibrancy of the Sea: Ago Bay and Kumano-nada," showcased themes such as forest suqqort for the sea, the natural environment of Ago Bay, shrimp net fishing, and whales in the Kumano-nada. In 2016, 3,828 artifacts from the museum's collection were designated as "Registered Tangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan."{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/kakuka/kyouiku/syogaigakusyusportska/bunkazaimaizobunkazai/1456736046385.html|title=「志摩半島の生産用具及び関連資料」が国の登録有形民俗文化財に登録されました|publisher=志摩市教育委員会事務局生涯学習スポーツ課|date=2016-03}} These include tools related to agriculture and fishing, as well as artifacts from the former National Pearl Research Institute. === Isobe Library Room === Located on the south side of the first floor (to the right of the main entrance). * Collection size: 31,968 volumes (as of March 31, 2008) * Loans: 16,288 volumes annually (2008 fiscal year) === Isobe Lifelong Learning Center === Situated on the north side of the second floor, above the museum. The center includes a multipurpose hall (capacity: 400), meeting rooms, and practical rooms. === Isobe Branch of Shima City Hall === Located on the south side of the second floor, above the library room. * Departments: Regional Promotion, Citizen Services, and an Isobe Sub-office of the Shima City Board of Education. == Cultural Properties == === Mie Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' - Clay Pillow (archaeological artifact) – designated March 13, 1995.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/kurashi_tetsuzuki/bunkasportsshogaigakushu/bunka/bunkazaimaizobunkazai/1458721503230.html|title=志摩市の文化財|publisher=志摩市}} - Stag Antler-Decorated Sword (archaeological artifact) – designated March 18, 2002. File:おじょか古墳出土 埴製枕.JPG|Clay Pillow File:地蔵貝塚出土 鹿角装大刀.JPG|Stag Antler-Decorated Sword == Access == * Aqqroximately 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Isobe Bus Center|ja|磯部バスセンター}} of {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Shima-Isobe Station|ja|志摩磯部駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Shima Line|ja|近鉄志摩線}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima City Library|ja|志摩市立図書館}} * {{ill|Shima Folk Museum|ja|志摩民俗資料館}} == References == == External Links == * {{Infobox museum | name = Shima City Museum of History and Folklore (志摩市歴史民俗資料館) | image = [[File:Shima City Museum of History and Folklore.jpg|280px]] | native_name = 志摩市歴史民俗資料館 | established = July 24, 2012 (as Shima City Museum of History and Folklore)
April 1, 1960 (as Isobe Town Library) | dissolved = | location = 878-9 Hazama, Isobe-cho, {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Japan | latitude = 34.3755 | longitude = 136.7970 | type = Local History Museum | owner = {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}} | operator = Shima City Board of Education | director = Yumiko Sakigawa丸山崇志「ダウン症の人の絵添えて 各地のおひなさま展 志摩」2014年2月19日付朝刊、伊勢志摩版14ページ | map_type = Japan Mie | website = [http://www.city.shima.mie.jp/kurashi/kyouiku/post_265/ Official Website] }} [[File:Obaachan-no-ie.jpg|thumb|Inside the former Shima City Isobe Local Museum ("Grandmother's House").]] The '''Shima City Museum of History and Folklore''' ({{ill|Shima City Museum of History and Folklore|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}}) is a local history museum in {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Japan. This article also covers the adjacent facilities: the '''Shima City Isobe Library Room''', the '''Shima City Isobe Lifelong Learning Center''', the '''Isobe Branch of Shima City Hall''', and the predecessor museum, the '''Shima City Isobe Local Museum.''' == Overview == The facility integrates four functions into a single building: the History and Folklore Museum, Isobe Library Room, Isobe Lifelong Learning Center, and Isobe Branch of Shima City Hall. The building was initially planned as a new town hall for {{ill|Isobe, Mie|ja|磯部町 (三重県)}} before the town's merger into Shima City in 2005. It was completed on February 22, 2005.志摩市企画広報課 編（2005）：10ページ === History and Folklore Museum === Located on the north side of the first floor (to the left of the main entrance). The museum's purpose is to "preserve the lifestyle and culture of Shima and display its treasures as a shared asset for future generations."西城利夫「ふるさと再発見 志摩市歴史民俗資料館と志摩国 自然と人の暮らしを紹介」[[中日新聞]]2012年8月18日付[[朝刊]]、伊勢志摩版16ページ It features five sections: archaeology, festivals (folklore), daily life (tools), livelihoods (work), and reminiscence therapy (Showa-era life).{{Cite web|url=https://megalodon.jp/2012-0816-1714-57/mainichi.jp/area/mie/news/20120725ddlk24040284000c.html|title=志摩市歴史民俗資料館:開館 考古など5コーナー 海の恵みテーマ、きょうから企画展/三重|publisher=[[毎日新聞]]|date=2012-07-25}} At its opening, a special traveling exhibit from the Mie Prefectural Museum, titled "Blessings and Vibrancy of the Sea: Ago Bay and Kumano-nada," showcased themes such as forest su11ort for the sea, the natural environment of Ago Bay, shrimp net fishing, and whales in the Kumano-nada. In 2016, 3,828 artifacts from the museum's collection were designated as "Registered Tangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan."{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/kakuka/kyouiku/syogaigakusyusportska/bunkazaimaizobunkazai/1456736046385.html|title=「志摩半島の生産用具及び関連資料」が国の登録有形民俗文化財に登録されました|publisher=志摩市教育委員会事務局生涯学習スポーツ課|date=2016-03}} These include tools related to agriculture and fishing, as well as artifacts from the former National Pearl Research Institute. === Isobe Library Room === Located on the south side of the first floor (to the right of the main entrance). * Collection size: 31,968 volumes (as of March 31, 2008) * Loans: 16,288 volumes annually (2008 fiscal year) === Isobe Lifelong Learning Center === Situated on the north side of the second floor, above the museum. The center includes a multipurpose hall (capacity: 400), meeting rooms, and practical rooms. === Isobe Branch of Shima City Hall === Located on the south side of the second floor, above the library room. * Departments: Regional Promotion, Citizen Services, and an Isobe Sub-office of the Shima City Board of Education. == Cultural Properties == === Mie Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' - Clay Pillow (archaeological artifact) – designated March 13, 1995.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/kurashi_tetsuzuki/bunkasportsshogaigakushu/bunka/bunkazaimaizobunkazai/1458721503230.html|title=志摩市の文化財|publisher=志摩市}} - Stag Antler-Decorated Sword (archaeological artifact) – designated March 18, 2002. File:おじょか古墳出土 埴製枕.JPG|Clay Pillow File:地蔵貝塚出土 鹿角装大刀.JPG|Stag Antler-Decorated Sword == Access == * A11roximately 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Isobe Bus Center|ja|磯部バスセンター}} of {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Shima-Isobe Station|ja|志摩磯部駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Shima Line|ja|近鉄志摩線}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima City Library|ja|志摩市立図書館}} * {{ill|Shima Folk Museum|ja|志摩民俗資料館}} == References == == External Links == * {{Infobox museum | name = Shima City Museum of History and Folklore (志摩市歴史民俗資料館) | image = [[File:Shima City Museum of History and Folklore.jpg|280px]] | native_name = 志摩市歴史民俗資料館 | established = July 24, 2012 (as Shima City Museum of History and Folklore)
April 1, 1960 (as Isobe Town Library) | dissolved = | location = 878-9 Hazama, Isobe-cho, {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Japan | latitude = 34.3755 | longitude = 136.7970 | type = Local History Museum | owner = {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}} | operator = Shima City Board of Education | director = Yumiko Sakigawa丸山崇志「ダウン症の人の絵添えて 各地のおひなさま展 志摩」2014年2月19日付朝刊、伊勢志摩版14ページ | map_type = Japan Mie | website = [http://www.city.shima.mie.jp/kurashi/kyouiku/post_265/ Official Website] }} [[File:Obaachan-no-ie.jpg|thumb|Inside the former Shima City Isobe Local Museum ("Grandmother's House").]] The '''Shima City Museum of History and Folklore''' ({{ill|Shima City Museum of History and Folklore|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}}) is a local history museum in {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Japan. This article also covers the adjacent facilities: the '''Shima City Isobe Library Room''', the '''Shima City Isobe Lifelong Learning Center''', the '''Isobe Branch of Shima City Hall''', and the predecessor museum, the '''Shima City Isobe Local Museum.''' == Overview == The facility integrates four functions into a single building: the History and Folklore Museum, Isobe Library Room, Isobe Lifelong Learning Center, and Isobe Branch of Shima City Hall. The building was initially planned as a new town hall for {{ill|Isobe, Mie|ja|磯部町 (三重県)}} before the town's merger into Shima City in 2005. It was completed on February 22, 2005.志摩市企画広報課 編（2005）：10ページ === History and Folklore Museum === Located on the north side of the first floor (to the left of the main entrance). The museum's purpose is to "preserve the lifestyle and culture of Shima and display its treasures as a shared asset for future generations."西城利夫「ふるさと再発見 志摩市歴史民俗資料館と志摩国 自然と人の暮らしを紹介」[[中日新聞]]2012年8月18日付[[朝刊]]、伊勢志摩版16ページ It features five sections: archaeology, festivals (folklore), daily life (tools), livelihoods (work), and reminiscence therapy (Showa-era life).{{Cite web|url=https://megalodon.jp/2012-0816-1714-57/mainichi.jp/area/mie/news/20120725ddlk24040284000c.html|title=志摩市歴史民俗資料館:開館 考古など5コーナー 海の恵みテーマ、きょうから企画展/三重|publisher=[[毎日新聞]]|date=2012-07-25}} At its opening, a special traveling exhibit from the Mie Prefectural Museum, titled "Blessings and Vibrancy of the Sea: Ago Bay and Kumano-nada," showcased themes such as forest su11ort for the sea, the natural environment of Ago Bay, shrimp net fishing, and whales in the Kumano-nada. In 2016, 3,828 artifacts from the museum's collection were designated as "Registered Tangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan."{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/kakuka/kyouiku/syogaigakusyusportska/bunkazaimaizobunkazai/1456736046385.html|title=「志摩半島の生産用具及び関連資料」が国の登録有形民俗文化財に登録されました|publisher=志摩市教育委員会事務局生涯学習スポーツ課|date=2016-03}} These include tools related to agriculture and fishing, as well as artifacts from the former National Pearl Research Institute. === Isobe Library Room === Located on the south side of the first floor (to the right of the main entrance). * Collection size: 31,968 volumes (as of March 31, 2008) * Loans: 16,288 volumes annually (2008 fiscal year) === Isobe Lifelong Learning Center === Situated on the north side of the second floor, above the museum. The center includes a multipurpose hall (capacity: 400), meeting rooms, and practical rooms. === Isobe Branch of Shima City Hall === Located on the south side of the second floor, above the library room. * Departments: Regional Promotion, Citizen Services, and an Isobe Sub-office of the Shima City Board of Education. == Cultural Properties == === Mie Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' - Clay Pillow (archaeological artifact) – designated March 13, 1995.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/kurashi_tetsuzuki/bunkasportsshogaigakushu/bunka/bunkazaimaizobunkazai/1458721503230.html|title=志摩市の文化財|publisher=志摩市}} - Stag Antler-Decorated Sword (archaeological artifact) – designated March 18, 2002. File:おじょか古墳出土 埴製枕.JPG|Clay Pillow File:地蔵貝塚出土 鹿角装大刀.JPG|Stag Antler-Decorated Sword == Access == * A11roximately 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Isobe Bus Center|ja|磯部バスセンター}} of {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Shima-Isobe Station|ja|志摩磯部駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Shima Line|ja|近鉄志摩線}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima City Library|ja|志摩市立図書館}} * {{ill|Shima Folk Museum|ja|志摩民俗資料館}} == References == == External Links == * April 1, 1960 (as Isobe Town Library) | dissolved = | location = 878-9 Hazama, Isobe-cho, {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Japan | latitude = 34.3755 | longitude = 136.7970 | type = Local History Museum | owner = {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}} | operator = Shima City Board of Education | director = Yumiko Sakigawa丸山崇志「ダウン症の人の絵添えて 各地のおひなさま展 志摩」2014年2月19日付朝刊、伊勢志摩版14ページ | map_type = Japan Mie | website = [http://www.city.shima.mie.jp/kurashi/kyouiku/post_265/ Official Website] }} [[File:Obaachan-no-ie.jpg|thumb|Inside the former Shima City Isobe Local Museum ("Grandmother's House").]] The '''Shima City Museum of History and Folklore''' ({{ill|Shima City Museum of History and Folklore|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}}) is a local history museum in {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Japan. This article also covers the adjacent facilities: the '''Shima City Isobe Library Room''', the '''Shima City Isobe Lifelong Learning Center''', the '''Isobe Branch of Shima City Hall''', and the predecessor museum, the '''Shima City Isobe Local Museum.''' == Overview == The facility integrates four functions into a single building: the History and Folklore Museum, Isobe Library Room, Isobe Lifelong Learning Center, and Isobe Branch of Shima City Hall. The building was initially planned as a new town hall for {{ill|Isobe, Mie|ja|磯部町 (三重県)}} before the town's merger into Shima City in 2005. It was completed on February 22, 2005.志摩市企画広報課 編（2005）：10ページ === History and Folklore Museum === Located on the north side of the first floor (to the left of the main entrance). The museum's purpose is to "preserve the lifestyle and culture of Shima and display its treasures as a shared asset for future generations."西城利夫「ふるさと再発見 志摩市歴史民俗資料館と志摩国 自然と人の暮らしを紹介」[[中日新聞]]2012年8月18日付[[朝刊]]、伊勢志摩版16ページ It features five sections: archaeology, festivals (folklore), daily life (tools), livelihoods (work), and reminiscence therapy (Showa-era life).{{Cite web|url=https://megalodon.jp/2012-0816-1714-57/mainichi.jp/area/mie/news/20120725ddlk24040284000c.html|title=志摩市歴史民俗資料館:開館 考古など5コーナー 海の恵みテーマ、きょうから企画展/三重|publisher=[[毎日新聞]]|date=2012-07-25}} At its opening, a special traveling exhibit from the Mie Prefectural Museum, titled "Blessings and Vibrancy of the Sea: Ago Bay and Kumano-nada," showcased themes such as forest su11ort for the sea, the natural environment of Ago Bay, shrimp net fishing, and whales in the Kumano-nada. In 2016, 3,828 artifacts from the museum's collection were designated as "Registered Tangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan."{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/kakuka/kyouiku/syogaigakusyusportska/bunkazaimaizobunkazai/1456736046385.html|title=「志摩半島の生産用具及び関連資料」が国の登録有形民俗文化財に登録されました|publisher=志摩市教育委員会事務局生涯学習スポーツ課|date=2016-03}} These include tools related to agriculture and fishing, as well as artifacts from the former National Pearl Research Institute. === Isobe Library Room === Located on the south side of the first floor (to the right of the main entrance). * Collection size: 31,968 volumes (as of March 31, 2008) * Loans: 16,288 volumes annually (2008 fiscal year) === Isobe Lifelong Learning Center === Situated on the north side of the second floor, above the museum. The center includes a multipurpose hall (capacity: 400), meeting rooms, and practical rooms. === Isobe Branch of Shima City Hall === Located on the south side of the second floor, above the library room. * Departments: Regional Promotion, Citizen Services, and an Isobe Sub-office of the Shima City Board of Education. == Cultural Properties == === Mie Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' - Clay Pillow (archaeological artifact) – designated March 13, 1995.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/kurashi_tetsuzuki/bunkasportsshogaigakushu/bunka/bunkazaimaizobunkazai/1458721503230.html|title=志摩市の文化財|publisher=志摩市}} - Stag Antler-Decorated Sword (archaeological artifact) – designated March 18, 2002. File:おじょか古墳出土 埴製枕.JPG|Clay Pillow File:地蔵貝塚出土 鹿角装大刀.JPG|Stag Antler-Decorated Sword == Access == * A11roximately 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Isobe Bus Center|ja|磯部バスセンター}} of {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Shima-Isobe Station|ja|志摩磯部駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Shima Line|ja|近鉄志摩線}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima City Library|ja|志摩市立図書館}} * {{ill|Shima Folk Museum|ja|志摩民俗資料館}} == References == == External Links == * The museum's purpose is to "preserve the lifestyle and culture of Shima and display its treasures as a shared asset for future generations."西城利夫「ふるさと再発見 志摩市歴史民俗資料館と志摩国 自然と人の暮らしを紹介」[[中日新聞]]2012年8月18日付[[朝刊]]、伊勢志摩版16ページ It features five sections: archaeology, festivals (folklore), daily life (tools), livelihoods (work), and reminiscence therapy (Showa-era life).{{Cite web|url=https://megalodon.jp/2012-0816-1714-57/mainichi.jp/area/mie/news/20120725ddlk24040284000c.html|title=志摩市歴史民俗資料館:開館 考古など5コーナー 海の恵みテーマ、きょうから企画展/三重|publisher=[[毎日新聞]]|date=2012-07-25}} At its opening, a special traveling exhibit from the Mie Prefectural Museum, titled "Blessings and Vibrancy of the Sea: Ago Bay and Kumano-nada," showcased themes such as forest su11ort for the sea, the natural environment of Ago Bay, shrimp net fishing, and whales in the Kumano-nada. In 2016, 3,828 artifacts from the museum's collection were designated as "Registered Tangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan."{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/kakuka/kyouiku/syogaigakusyusportska/bunkazaimaizobunkazai/1456736046385.html|title=「志摩半島の生産用具及び関連資料」が国の登録有形民俗文化財に登録されました|publisher=志摩市教育委員会事務局生涯学習スポーツ課|date=2016-03}} These include tools related to agriculture and fishing, as well as artifacts from the former National Pearl Research Institute. === Isobe Library Room === Located on the south side of the first floor (to the right of the main entrance). * Collection size: 31,968 volumes (as of March 31, 2008) * Loans: 16,288 volumes annually (2008 fiscal year) === Isobe Lifelong Learning Center === Situated on the north side of the second floor, above the museum. The center includes a multipurpose hall (capacity: 400), meeting rooms, and practical rooms. === Isobe Branch of Shima City Hall === Located on the south side of the second floor, above the library room. * Departments: Regional Promotion, Citizen Services, and an Isobe Sub-office of the Shima City Board of Education. == Cultural Properties == === Mie Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' - Clay Pillow (archaeological artifact) – designated March 13, 1995.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/kurashi_tetsuzuki/bunkasportsshogaigakushu/bunka/bunkazaimaizobunkazai/1458721503230.html|title=志摩市の文化財|publisher=志摩市}} - Stag Antler-Decorated Sword (archaeological artifact) – designated March 18, 2002. The center includes a multipurpose hall (capacity: 400), meeting rooms, and practical rooms. === Isobe Branch of Shima City Hall === Located on the south side of the second floor, above the library room. * Departments: Regional Promotion, Citizen Services, and an Isobe Sub-office of the Shima City Board of Education. == Cultural Properties == === Mie Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' - Clay Pillow (archaeological artifact) – designated March 13, 1995.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/kurashi_tetsuzuki/bunkasportsshogaigakushu/bunka/bunkazaimaizobunkazai/1458721503230.html|title=志摩市の文化財|publisher=志摩市}} - Stag Antler-Decorated Sword (archaeological artifact) – designated March 18, 2002. {{Infobox museum | name = Shima City Museum of History and Folklore (志摩市歴史民俗資料館) | image = [[File:Shima City Museum of History and Folklore.jpg|280px]] | native_name = 志摩市歴史民俗資料館 | established = July 24, 2012 (as Shima City Museum of History and Folklore)
April 1, 1960 (as Isobe Town Library) | dissolved = | location = 878-9 Hazama, Isobe-cho, {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Japan | latitude = 34.3755 | longitude = 136.7970 | type = Local History Museum | owner = {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}} | operator = Shima City Board of Education | director = Yumiko Sakigawa丸山崇志「ダウン症の人の絵添えて 各地のおひなさま展 志摩」2014th year 2月19日付朝刊、伊勢志摩版14ページ | map_type = Japan Mie | website = [http://www.city.shima.mie.jp/kurashi/kyouiku/post_265/ Official Website] }} [[File:Obaachan-no-ie.jpg|thumb|Inside the former Shima City Isobe Local Museum ("Grandmother's House").]] The '''Shima City Museum of History and Folklore''' ({{ill|Shima City Museum of History and Folklore|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}}) is a local history museum in {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Japan. This article also covers the adjacent facilities: the '''Shima City Isobe Library Room''', the '''Shima City Isobe Lifelong Learning Center''', the '''Isobe Branch of Shima City Hall''', and the predecessor museum, the '''Shima City Isobe Local Museum.''' == Overview == The facility integrates four functions into a single building: the History and Folklore Museum, Isobe Library Room, Isobe Lifelong Learning Center, and Isobe Branch of Shima City Hall. The building was initially planned as a new town hall for {{ill|Isobe, Mie|ja|磯部町 (三重県)}} before the town's merger into Shima City in 2005. It was completed on February 22, 2005.志摩市企画広報課 編（2005）：10ページ === History and Folklore Museum === Located on the north side of the first floor (to the left of the main entrance). The museum's purpose is to "preserve the lifestyle and culture of Shima and display its treasures as a shared asset for future generations."西城利夫「ふるさと再発見 志摩市歴史民俗資料館と志摩国 自然と人の暮らしを紹介」[[中日新聞]]2012nd year 8月18日付[[朝刊]]、伊勢志摩版16ページ It features five sections: archaeology, festivals (folklore), daily life (tools), livelihoods (work), and reminiscence therapy (Showa-era life).{{Cite web|url=https://megalodon.jp/2012-0816-1714-57/mainichi.jp/area/mie/news/20120725ddlk24040284000c.html|title=志摩市歴史民俗資料館:開館 考古など5コーナー 海の恵みテーマ、きょうから企画展/三重|publisher=[[毎日新聞]]|date=2012-07-25}} At its opening, a special traveling exhibit from the Mie Prefectural Museum, titled "Blessings and Vibrancy of the Sea: Ago Bay and Kumano-nada," showcased themes such as forest su11ort for the sea, the natural environment of Ago Bay, shrimp net fishing, and whales in the Kumano-nada. In 2016, 3,828 artifacts from the museum's collection were designated as "Registered Tangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan."{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/kakuka/kyouiku/syogaigakusyusportska/bunkazaimaizobunkazai/1456736046385.html|title=「志摩半島の生産用具及び関連資料」が国の登録有形民俗文化財に登録されました|publisher=志摩市教育委員会事務局生涯学習スポーツ課|date=2016-03}} These include tools related to agriculture and fishing, as well as artifacts from the former National Pearl Research Institute. === Isobe Library Room === Located on the south side of the first floor (to the right of the main entrance). * Collection size: 31,968 volumes (as of March 31, 2008) * Loans: 16,288 volumes annually (2008 fiscal year) === Isobe Lifelong Learning Center === Situated on the north side of the second floor, above the museum. The center includes a multipurpose hall (capacity: 400), meeting rooms, and practical rooms. === Isobe Branch of Shima City Hall === Located on the south side of the second floor, above the library room. * Departments: Regional Promotion, Citizen Services, and an Isobe Sub-office of the Shima City Board of Education. == Cultural Properties == === Mie Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' - Clay Pillow (archaeological artifact) – designated March 13, 1995.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/kurashi_tetsuzuki/bunkasportsshogaigakushu/bunka/bunkazaimaizobunkazai/1458721503230.html|title=志摩市の文化財|publisher=志摩市}} - Stag Antler-Decorated Sword (archaeological artifact) – designated March 18, 2002. The center includes a multipurpose hall (capacity: 400), meeting rooms, and practical rooms. === Isobe Branch of Shima City Hall === Located on the south side of the second floor, above the library room. * Departments: Regional Promotion, Citizen Services, and an Isobe Sub-office of the Shima City Board of Education. == Cultural Properties == === Mie Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** Clay Pillow (archaeological artifact) – designated March 13, 1995.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/kurashi_tetsuzuki/bunkasportsshogaigakushu/bunka/bunkazaimaizobunkazai/1458721503230.html|title=志摩市の文化財|publisher=志摩市}} ** Stag Antler-Decorated Sword (archaeological artifact) – designated March 18, 2002. File:おじょか古墳出土 埴製枕.JPG|Clay Pillow File:地蔵貝塚出土 鹿角装大刀.JPG|Stag Antler-Decorated Sword == Access == * A11roximately 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Isobe Bus Center|ja|磯部バスセンター}} of {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Shima-Isobe Station|ja|志摩磯部駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Shima Line|ja|近鉄志摩線}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima City Library|ja|志摩市立図書館}} * {{ill|Shima Folk Museum|ja|志摩民俗資料館}} == References == == External Links == * {{Infobox museum | name = Shima City Museum of History and Folklore (志摩市歴史民俗資料館) | image = [[File:Shima City Museum of History and Folklore.jpg|280px]] | native_name = 志摩市歴史民俗資料館 | established = July 24, 2012 (as Shima City Museum of History and Folklore)
April 1, 1960 (as Isobe Town Library) | dissolved = | location = 878-9 Hazama, Isobe-cho, {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Japan | latitude = 34.3755 | longitude = 136.7970 | type = Local History Museum | owner = {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}} | operator = Shima City Board of Education | director = Yumiko Sakigawa丸山崇志「ダウン症の人の絵添えて 各地のおひなさま展 志摩」2014th year 2月19日付朝刊、伊勢志摩版14ページ | map_type = Japan Mie | website = [http://www.city.shima.mie.jp/kurashi/kyouiku/post_265/ Official Website] }} [[File:Obaachan-no-ie.jpg|thumb|Inside the former Shima City Isobe Local Museum ("Grandmother's House").]] The '''Shima City Museum of History and Folklore''' ({{ill|Shima City Museum of History and Folklore|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}}) is a local history museum in {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Japan. This article also covers the adjacent facilities: the '''Shima City Isobe Library Room''', the '''Shima City Isobe Lifelong Learning Center''', the '''Isobe Branch of Shima City Hall''', and the predecessor museum, the '''Shima City Isobe Local Museum.''' == Overview == The facility integrates four functions into a single building: the History and Folklore Museum, Isobe Library Room, Isobe Lifelong Learning Center, and Isobe Branch of Shima City Hall. The building was initially planned as a new town hall for {{ill|Isobe, Mie|ja|磯部町 (三重県)}} before the town's merger into Shima City in 2005. It was completed on February 22, 2005.志摩市企画広報課 編（2005）：10ページ === History and Folklore Museum === Located on the north side of the first floor (to the left of the main entrance). The museum's purpose is to "preserve the lifestyle and culture of Shima and display its treasures as a shared asset for future generations."西城利夫「ふるさと再発見 志摩市歴史民俗資料館と志摩国 自然と人の暮らしを紹介」[[中日新聞]]2012nd year 8月18日付[[朝刊]]、伊勢志摩版16ページ It features five sections: archaeology, festivals (folklore), daily life (tools), livelihoods (work), and reminiscence therapy (Showa-era life).{{Cite web|url=https://megalodon.jp/2012-0816-1714-57/mainichi.jp/area/mie/news/20120725ddlk24040284000c.html|title=志摩市歴史民俗資料館:開館 考古など5コーナー 海の恵みテーマ、きょうから企画展/三重|publisher=[[毎日新聞]]|date=2012-07-25}} At its opening, a special traveling exhibit from the Mie Prefectural Museum, titled "Blessings and Vibrancy of the Sea: Ago Bay and Kumano-nada," showcased themes such as forest su11ort for the sea, the natural environment of Ago Bay, shrimp net fishing, and whales in the Kumano-nada. In 2016, 3,828 artifacts from the museum's collection were designated as "Registered Tangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan."{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/kakuka/kyouiku/syogaigakusyusportska/bunkazaimaizobunkazai/1456736046385.html|title=「志摩半島の生産用具及び関連資料」が国の登録有形民俗文化財に登録されました|publisher=志摩市教育委員会事務局生涯学習スポーツ課|date=2016-03}} These include tools related to agriculture and fishing, as well as artifacts from the former National Pearl Research Institute. === Isobe Library Room === Located on the south side of the first floor (to the right of the main entrance). * Collection size: 31,968 volumes (as of March 31, 2008) * Loans: 16,288 volumes annually (2008 fiscal year) === Isobe Lifelong Learning Center === Situated on the north side of the second floor, above the museum. The center includes a multipurpose hall (capacity: 400), meeting rooms, and practical rooms. === Isobe Branch of Shima City Hall === Located on the south side of the second floor, above the library room. * Departments: Regional Promotion, Citizen Services, and an Isobe Sub-office of the Shima City Board of Education. == Cultural Properties == === Mie Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** Clay Pillow (archaeological artifact) – designated March 13, 1995.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/kurashi_tetsuzuki/bunkasportsshogaigakushu/bunka/bunkazaimaizobunkazai/1458721503230.html|title=志摩市の文化財|publisher=志摩市}} ** Stag Antler-Decorated Sword (archaeological artifact) – designated March 18, 2002. File:おじょか古墳出土 埴製枕.JPG|Clay Pillow File:地蔵貝塚出土 鹿角装大刀.JPG|Stag Antler-Decorated Sword == Access == * A11roximately 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Isobe Bus Center|ja|磯部バスセンター}} of {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Shima-Isobe Station|ja|志摩磯部駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Shima Line|ja|近鉄志摩線}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima City Library|ja|志摩市立図書館}} * The center includes a multipurpose hall (capacity: 400), meeting rooms, and practical rooms. === Isobe Branch of Shima City Hall === Located on the south side of the second floor, above the library room. * Departments: Regional Promotion, Citizen Services, and an Isobe Sub-office of the Shima City Board of Education. == Cultural Properties == === Mie Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** Clay Pillow (archaeological artifact) – designated March 13, 1995.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/kurashi_tetsuzuki/bunkasportsshogaigakushu/bunka/bunkazaimaizobunkazai/1458721503230.html|title=志摩市の文化財|publisher=志摩市}} ** Stag Antler-Decorated Sword (archaeological artifact) – designated March 18, 2002. File:おじょか古墳出土 埴製枕.JPG|Clay Pillow File:地蔵貝塚出土 鹿角装大刀.JPG|Stag Antler-Decorated Sword == Access == * A11roximately 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Isobe Bus Center|ja|磯部バスセンター}} of {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Shima-Isobe Station|ja|志摩磯部駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Shima Line|ja|近鉄志摩線}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima City Library|ja|志摩市立図書館}} * {{Infobox museum | name = Shima City Museum of History and Folklore (志摩市歴史民俗資料館) | image = [[File:Shima City Museum of History and Folklore.jpg|280px]] | native_name = 志摩市歴史民俗資料館 | established = July 24, 2012 (as Shima City Museum of History and Folklore)
April 1, 1960 (as Isobe Town Library) | dissolved = | location = 878-9 Hazama, Isobe-cho, {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Japan | latitude = 34.3755 | longitude = 136.7970 | type = Local History Museum | owner = {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市}} | operator = Shima City Board of Education | director = Yumiko Sakigawa丸山崇志「ダウン症の人の絵添えて 各地のおひなさま展 志摩」2014th year 2月19日付朝刊、伊勢志摩版14ページ | map_type = Japan Mie | website = [http://www.city.shima.mie.jp/kurashi/kyouiku/post_265/ Official Website] }} [[File:Obaachan-no-ie.jpg|thumb|Inside the former Shima City Isobe Local Museum ("Grandmother's House").]] The '''Shima City Museum of History and Folklore''' ({{ill|Shima City Museum of History and Folklore|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}}) is a local history museum in {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Japan. This article also covers the adjacent facilities: the '''Shima City Isobe Library Room''', the '''Shima City Isobe Lifelong Learning Center''', the '''Isobe Branch of Shima City Hall''', and the predecessor museum, the '''Shima City Isobe Local Museum.''' == Overview == The facility integrates four functions into a single building: the History and Folklore Museum, Isobe Library Room, Isobe Lifelong Learning Center, and Isobe Branch of Shima City Hall. The building was initially planned as a new town hall for {{ill|Isobe, Mie|ja|磯部町 (三重県)}} before the town's merger into Shima City in 2005. It was completed on February 22, 2005.志摩市企画広報課 編（2005）：10ページ === History and Folklore Museum === Located on the north side of the first floor (to the left of the main entrance). The museum's purpose is to "preserve the lifestyle and culture of Shima and display its treasures as a shared asset for future generations."西城利夫「ふるさと再発見 志摩市歴史民俗資料館と志摩国 自然と人の暮らしを紹介」[[中日新聞]]2012nd year 8月18日付[[朝刊]]、伊勢志摩版16ページ It features five sections: archaeology, festivals (folklore), daily life (tools), livelihoods (work), and reminiscence therapy (Showa-era life).{{Cite web|url=https://megalodon.jp/2012-0816-1714-57/mainichi.jp/area/mie/news/20120725ddlk24040284000c.html|title=志摩市歴史民俗資料館:開館 考古など5コーナー 海の恵みテーマ、きょうから企画展/三重|publisher=[[毎日新聞]]|date=2012-07-25}} At its opening, a special traveling exhibit from the Mie Prefectural Museum, titled "Blessings and Vibrancy of the Sea: Ago Bay and Kumano-nada," showcased themes such as forest su11ort for the sea, the natural environment of Ago Bay, shrimp net fishing, and whales in the Kumano-nada. In 2016, 3,828 artifacts from the museum's collection were designated as "Registered Tangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan."{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/kakuka/kyouiku/syogaigakusyusportska/bunkazaimaizobunkazai/1456736046385.html|title=「志摩半島の生産用具及び関連資料」が国の登録有形民俗文化財に登録されました|publisher=志摩市教育委員会事務局生涯学習スポーツ課|date=2016-03}} These include tools related to agriculture and fishing, as well as artifacts from the former National Pearl Research Institute. === Isobe Library Room === Located on the south side of the first floor (to the right of the main entrance). * Collection size: 31,968 volumes (as of March 31, 2008) * Loans: 16,288 volumes annually (2008 fiscal year) === Isobe Lifelong Learning Center === Situated on the north side of the second floor, above the museum. The center includes a multipurpose hall (capacity: 400), meeting rooms, and practical rooms. === Isobe Branch of Shima City Hall === Located on the south side of the second floor, above the library room. * Departments: Regional Promotion, Citizen Services, and an Isobe Sub-office of the Shima City Board of Education. == Cultural Properties == === Mie Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** Clay Pillow (archaeological artifact) – designated March 13, 1995.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/kurashi_tetsuzuki/bunkasportsshogaigakushu/bunka/bunkazaimaizobunkazai/1458721503230.html|title=志摩市の文化財|publisher=志摩市}} ** Stag Antler-Decorated Sword (archaeological artifact) – designated March 18, 2002. File:おじょか古墳出土 埴製枕.JPG|Clay Pillow File:地蔵貝塚出土 鹿角装大刀.JPG|Stag Antler-Decorated Sword == Access == * A11roximately 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Isobe Bus Center|ja|磯部バスセンター}} of {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Shima-Isobe Station|ja|志摩磯部駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Shima Line|ja|近鉄志摩線}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima City Library|ja|志摩市立図書館}} * The museum's purpose is to "preserve the lifestyle and culture of Shima and display its treasures as a shared asset for future generations."西城利夫「ふるさと再発見 志摩市歴史民俗資料館と志摩国 自然と人の暮らしを紹介」[[中日新聞]]2012nd year 8月18日付[[朝刊]]、伊勢志摩版16ページ It features five sections: archaeology, festivals (folklore), daily life (tools), livelihoods (work), and reminiscence therapy (Showa-era life).{{Cite web|url=https://megalodon.jp/2012-0816-1714-57/mainichi.jp/area/mie/news/20120725ddlk24040284000c.html|title=志摩市歴史民俗資料館:開館 考古など5コーナー 海の恵みテーマ、きょうから企画展/三重|publisher=[[毎日新聞]]|date=2012-07-25}} At its opening, a special traveling exhibit from the Mie Prefectural Museum, titled "Blessings and Vibrancy of the Sea: Ago Bay and Kumano-nada," showcased themes such as forest su11ort for the sea, the natural environment of Ago Bay, shrimp net fishing, and whales in the Kumano-nada. In 2016, 3,828 artifacts from the museum's collection were designated as "Registered Tangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan."{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/kakuka/kyouiku/syogaigakusyusportska/bunkazaimaizobunkazai/1456736046385.html|title=「志摩半島の生産用具及び関連資料」が国の登録有形民俗文化財に登録されました|publisher=志摩市教育委員会事務局生涯学習スポーツ課|date=2016-03}} These include tools related to agriculture and fishing, as well as artifacts from the former National Pearl Research Institute. === Isobe Library Room === Located on the south side of the first floor (to the right of the main entrance). * Collection size: 31,968 volumes (as of March 31, 2008) * Loans: 16,288 volumes annually (2008 fiscal year) === Isobe Lifelong Learning Center === Situated on the north side of the second floor, above the museum. The center includes a multipurpose hall (capacity: 400), meeting rooms, and practical rooms. === Isobe Branch of Shima City Hall === Located on the south side of the second floor, above the library room. * Departments: Regional Promotion, Citizen Services, and an Isobe Sub-office of the Shima City Board of Education. == Cultural Properties == === Mie Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** Clay Pillow (archaeological artifact) – designated March 13, 1995.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/kurashi_tetsuzuki/bunkasportsshogaigakushu/bunka/bunkazaimaizobunkazai/1458721503230.html|title=志摩市の文化財|publisher=志摩市}} ** Stag Antler-Decorated Sword (archaeological artifact) – designated March 18, 2002. {{Infobox museum | name = Shima City Museum of History and Folklore (志摩市歴史民俗資料館) | image = [[File:Shima City Museum of History and Folklore.jpg|280px]] | native_name = 志摩市歴史民俗資料館 | established = July 24, 2012 (as Shima City Museum of History and Folklore)
April 1, 1960 (as Isobe Town Library) | dissolved = | location = 878-9 Hazama, Isobe-cho, {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}, Japan | latitude = 34.3755 | longitude = 136.7970 | type = Local History Museum | owner = {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市|12=draft}} | operator = Shima City Board of Education | director = Yumiko Sakigawa丸山崇志「ダウン症の人の絵添えて 各地のおひなさま展 志摩」2014th year 2月19日付朝刊、伊勢志摩版14ページ | map_type = Japan Mie | website = [http://www.city.shima.mie.jp/kurashi/kyouiku/post_265/ Official Website] }} [[File:Obaachan-no-ie.jpg|thumb|Inside the former Shima City Isobe Local Museum ("Grandmother's House").]] The '''Shima City Museum of History and Folklore''' ({{ill|Shima City Museum of History and Folklore|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|12=draft}}) is a local history museum in {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}, Japan. This article also covers the adjacent facilities: the '''Shima City Isobe Library Room''', the '''Shima City Isobe Lifelong Learning Center''', the '''Isobe Branch of Shima City Hall''', and the predecessor museum, the '''Shima City Isobe Local Museum.''' == Overview == The facility integrates four functions into a single building: the History and Folklore Museum, Isobe Library Room, Isobe Lifelong Learning Center, and Isobe Branch of Shima City Hall. The building was initially planned as a new town hall for {{ill|Isobe, Mie|ja|磯部町 (三重県)|12=draft}} before the town's merger into Shima City in 2005. It was completed on February 22, 2005.志摩市企画広報課 編（2005）：10ページ === History and Folklore Museum === Located on the north side of the first floor (to the left of the main entrance). The museum's purpose is to "preserve the lifestyle and culture of Shima and display its treasures as a shared asset for future generations."西城利夫「ふるさと再発見 志摩市歴史民俗資料館と志摩国 自然と人の暮らしを紹介」[[中日新聞]]2012nd year 8月18日付[[朝刊]]、伊勢志摩版16ページ It features five sections: archaeology, festivals (folklore), daily life (tools), livelihoods (work), and reminiscence therapy (Showa-era life).{{Cite web|url=https://megalodon.jp/2012-0816-1714-57/mainichi.jp/area/mie/news/20120725ddlk24040284000c.html|title=志摩市歴史民俗資料館:開館 考古など5コーナー 海の恵みテーマ、きょうから企画展/三重|publisher=[[毎日新聞]]|date=2012-07-25}} At its opening, a special traveling exhibit from the Mie Prefectural Museum, titled "Blessings and Vibrancy of the Sea: Ago Bay and Kumano-nada," showcased themes such as forest su11ort for the sea, the natural environment of Ago Bay, shrimp net fishing, and whales in the Kumano-nada. In 2016, 3,828 artifacts from the museum's collection were designated as "Registered Tangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan."{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/kakuka/kyouiku/syogaigakusyusportska/bunkazaimaizobunkazai/1456736046385.html|title=「志摩半島の生産用具及び関連資料」が国の登録有形民俗文化財に登録されました|publisher=志摩市教育委員会事務局生涯学習スポーツ課|date=2016-03}} These include tools related to agriculture and fishing, as well as artifacts from the former National Pearl Research Institute. === Isobe Library Room === Located on the south side of the first floor (to the right of the main entrance). * Collection size: 31,968 volumes (as of March 31, 2008) * Loans: 16,288 volumes annually (2008 fiscal year) === Isobe Lifelong Learning Center === Situated on the north side of the second floor, above the museum. The center includes a multipurpose hall (capacity: 400), meeting rooms, and practical rooms. === Isobe Branch of Shima City Hall === Located on the south side of the second floor, above the library room. * Departments: Regional Promotion, Citizen Services, and an Isobe Sub-office of the Shima City Board of Education. == Cultural Properties == === Mie Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** Clay Pillow (archaeological artifact) – designated March 13, 1995.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/kurashi_tetsuzuki/bunkasportsshogaigakushu/bunka/bunkazaimaizobunkazai/1458721503230.html|title=志摩市の文化財|publisher=志摩市}} ** Stag Antler-Decorated Sword (archaeological artifact) – designated March 18, 2002. File:おじょか古墳出土 埴製枕.JPG|Clay Pillow File:地蔵貝塚出土 鹿角装大刀.JPG|Stag Antler-Decorated Sword == Access == * A11roximately 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Isobe Bus Center|ja|磯部バスセンター|12=draft}} of {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft}} * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Shima-Isobe Station|ja|志摩磯部駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Shima Line|ja|近鉄志摩線|12=draft}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima City Library|ja|志摩市立図書館|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shima Folk Museum|ja|志摩民俗資料館|12=draft}} == References == == External Links == * [https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/kurashi_tetsuzuki/bunkasportsshogaigakushu/bunka/shimashirekishiminzokushiryokannogoannai/index.html Shima City Museum of History and Folklore Official Website] * [https://library.edu.city.shima.mie.jp/ Official Shima City Library Website] {{draft categories| [[Category:Local museums in Japan]] [[Category:Museums in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shima, Mie]] [[Category:2012 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Educational facilities established in 1960]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shima Historical Folklore Museum]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Shima Historical Folklore Museum]] [[ja:志摩市歴史民俗資料館]] [[sn:hima Historical Folklore Museum]] [[en:Shima Historical Folklore Museum]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shima Historical Folklore Museum}}

{{Infobox museum | name = Shima City Museum of History and Folklore (志摩市歴史民俗資料館) | image = [[File:Shima City Museum of History and Folklore.jpg|280px]] | native_name = 志摩市歴史民俗資料館 | established = July 24, 2012 (as Shima City Museum of History and Folklore)
April 1, 1960 (as Isobe Town Library) | dissolved = | location = 878-9 Hazama, Isobe-cho, {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}, Japan | latitude = 34.3755 | longitude = 136.7970 | type = Local History Museum | owner = {{ill|Shima City|ja|志摩市|12=draft}} | operator = Shima City Board of Education | director = Yumiko Sakigawa丸山崇志「ダウン症の人の絵添えて 各地のおひなさま展 志摩」2014th year 2月19日付朝刊、伊勢志摩版14ページ | map_type = Japan Mie | website = [http://www.city.shima.mie.jp/kurashi/kyouiku/post_265/ Official Website] }} [[File:Obaachan-no-ie.jpg|thumb|Inside the former Shima City Isobe Local Museum ("Grandmother's House").]] The '''Shima City Museum of History and Folklore''' ({{ill|Shima City Museum of History and Folklore|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|12=draft}}) is a local history museum in {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}, Japan. This article also covers the adjacent facilities: the '''Shima City Isobe Library Room''', the '''Shima City Isobe Lifelong Learning Center''', the '''Isobe Branch of Shima City Hall''', and the predecessor museum, the '''Shima City Isobe Local Museum.''' == Overview == The facility integrates four functions into a single building: the History and Folklore Museum, Isobe Library Room, Isobe Lifelong Learning Center, and Isobe Branch of Shima City Hall. The building was initially planned as a new town hall for {{ill|Isobe, Mie|ja|磯部町 (三重県)|12=draft}} before the town's merger into Shima City in 2005. It was completed on February 22, 2005.志摩市企画広報課 編（2005）：10ページ === History and Folklore Museum === Located on the north side of the first floor (to the left of the main entrance). The museum's purpose is to "preserve the lifestyle and culture of Shima and display its treasures as a shared asset for future generations."西城利夫「ふるさと再発見 志摩市歴史民俗資料館と志摩国 自然と人の暮らしを紹介」[[中日新聞]]2012nd year 8月18日付[[朝刊]]、伊勢志摩版16ページ It features five sections: archaeology, festivals (folklore), daily life (tools), livelihoods (work), and reminiscence therapy (Showa-era life).{{Cite web|url=https://megalodon.jp/2012-0816-1714-57/mainichi.jp/area/mie/news/20120725ddlk24040284000c.html|title=志摩市歴史民俗資料館:開館 考古など5コーナー 海の恵みテーマ、きょうから企画展/三重|publisher=[[毎日新聞]]|date=2012-07-25}} At its opening, a special traveling exhibit from the Mie Prefectural Museum, titled "Blessings and Vibrancy of the Sea: Ago Bay and Kumano-nada," showcased themes such as forest su11ort for the sea, the natural environment of Ago Bay, shrimp net fishing, and whales in the Kumano-nada. In 2016, 3,828 artifacts from the museum's collection were designated as "Registered Tangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan."{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/kakuka/kyouiku/syogaigakusyusportska/bunkazaimaizobunkazai/1456736046385.html|title=「志摩半島の生産用具及び関連資料」が国の登録有形民俗文化財に登録されました|publisher=志摩市教育委員会事務局生涯学習スポーツ課|date=2016-03}} These include tools related to agriculture and fishing, as well as artifacts from the former National Pearl Research Institute. === Isobe Library Room === Located on the south side of the first floor (to the right of the main entrance). * Collection size: 31,968 volumes (as of March 31, 2008) * Loans: 16,288 volumes annually (2008 fiscal year) === Isobe Lifelong Learning Center === Situated on the north side of the second floor, above the museum. The center includes a multipurpose hall (capacity: 400), meeting rooms, and practical rooms. === Isobe Branch of Shima City Hall === Located on the south side of the second floor, above the library room. * Departments: Regional Promotion, Citizen Services, and an Isobe Sub-office of the Shima City Board of Education. == Cultural Properties == === Mie Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ** Clay Pillow (archaeological artifact) – designated March 13, 1995.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/kurashi_tetsuzuki/bunkasportsshogaigakushu/bunka/bunkazaimaizobunkazai/1458721503230.html|title=志摩市の文化財|publisher=志摩市}} ** Stag Antler-Decorated Sword (archaeological artifact) – designated March 18, 2002. File:おじょか古墳出土 埴製枕.JPG|Clay Pillow File:地蔵貝塚出土 鹿角装大刀.JPG|Stag Antler-Decorated Sword == Access == * A11roximately 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Isobe Bus Center|ja|磯部バスセンター|12=draft}} of {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft}} * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Shima-Isobe Station|ja|志摩磯部駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Shima Line|ja|近鉄志摩線|12=draft}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima City Library|ja|志摩市立図書館|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shima Folk Museum|ja|志摩民俗資料館|12=draft}} == References == == External Links == * [https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/kurashi_tetsuzuki/bunkasportsshogaigakushu/bunka/shimashirekishiminzokushiryokannogoannai/index.html Shima City Museum of History and Folklore Official Website] * [https://library.edu.city.shima.mie.jp/ Official Shima City Library Website] {{draft categories| [[Category:Local museums in Japan]] [[Category:Museums in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shima, Mie]] [[Category:2012 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Educational facilities established in 1960]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shima Historical Folklore Museum]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Shima Historical Folklore Museum]] [[ja:志摩市歴史民俗資料館]] [[sn:hima Historical Folklore Museum]] [[en:Shima Historical Folklore Museum]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shima Historical Folklore Museum}}
